default_bullets.text: Meet with Target's officers and management in order to develop an understanding of operations, including its:

children.text: Basis of financial information;
Organization structure;
Historical growth by geography; and
Finance function, financial reporting framework and internal control environment.

default_bullets.text: Read Target's financial statements and discuss Target's accounting policies and practices with management, including:

children.text: Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships (including processes, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/accounting systems);
Significant accounting policies and recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures, or estimates;
Significant accounting estimates (e.g. bad debt provision, rebates, capitalized R&D, etc.); and
Intercompany accounts and related party transactions, if any.

default_bullets.text: Consider if the following accounting policies have been applied consistently within and across historical periods:

children.text: Cash versus accrual accounting policies;
Capitalization policies;
Depreciation policies; and
Differences between interim and year-end procedures; and
Revenue recognition policies;

default_bullets.text: Identify, and where possible quantify, potential earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization ("EBITDA") normalization items by considering:

children.text: The impact of the normalization adjustments identified by Management;
Large, unusual or non-recurring events, or transactions that may have distorted results;
The impact of any changes to senior management or management team structure;
The impact of provisions (allowance for doubtful accounts, inventory obsolescence, other), management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
The impact of foreign currency transactions;
The impact of related party transactions and shared services provided by other entities, if applicable;
The impact of any changes in accounting policies, procedures and estimates; and
Other potential items identified during the due diligence process.

default_bullets.text: Obtain a comprehensive view of revenue and margins. Obtain the underlying transactional data (i.e. sales database), if available, and gain an understanding of the major trends in revenue and margins addressing areas including:

children.text: Revenue and margin by significant customer and end market/ segment/ region;
Price and volume trends.
impact of large or long-term orders/ seasonality (if applicable); and
any non-recurring components of revenue.
customer category (i.e. individual, business);
distribution channel (i.e. online, wholesale, etc.); and
price, volume, mix and cross-sell analysis.
Seasonal fluctuations in demand and monthly sales trends; and

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's revenue and inquire about:

children.text: Gross to net revenue;
Revenue recognition policies;
Revenue and gross margin by customer;
Organic growth and delineation between price vs. volume;
Pricing practices;
Promotions and discounts;
Any non-recurring components of revenue;
Revenue seasonality, if any;
Other components of cost of sales and key suppliers.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Costing methodology;
Cost of sale trends, impact of material change in vendors (if any);
Employee compensation and related costs by function;
Selling, general, and administrative expenses;
Repairs and maintenance expense;
Unusual and extraordinary items (if any).

default_bullets.text: Obtain monthly details of the Company's consolidated working capital and analyze and comment on:

children.text: The composition of individual working capital accounts (e.g., trade receivables, accounts payable and accruals, etc.);
Monthly working capital trends, metrics, and seasonality;
Large, unusual, or non-operating items that may have affected normal working capital trends, (such as accruals for bonuses, capital accruals, month end vs quarter end differences, etc.); and
Understanding the accounting impact of allowance for uncollectible amounts and other accruals requiring judgment.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of the Target’s accounts receivable and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Billed/unbilled aging analysis, turnover and days sales outstanding;
Credit terms;
Trade and non-trade balances;
Allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of accounts payable, accrued liabilities and customer deposits and inquire about:

children.text: Accounts payable aging and days outstanding;
Accrued liabilities;
Supplier settlement terms; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of existing and future capital cost requirements including:

children.text: Maintenance versus growth capital expenditures;
Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures

default_bullets.text: Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:

children.text: Pending or threatened litigation or investigations by regulatory or other authorities; and
Contractual obligations, including leases;
Purchase commitments; and
Employee agreements (e.g. transaction/retention bonuses, change-in-control, deferred compensation, or severance agreements, etc.);
Incentive compensation and employee benefit obligations;
Expected or contingent liabilities (e.g. environmental, litigation, regulatory and tax); and
Other off-balance sheet transactions.

default_bullets.text: Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off-balance sheet) presented by Management and other potential debt-like items;

Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt; and

financial_due_diligence.text: The deliverable will be in the form of a report and Excel data book.

Unless otherwise noted, our work will concentrate on financial information for the last three fiscal years ended the most recent interim period, along with the most recent available financial information (e.g. year-to-date the most recent interim period or more recent monthly data as available).

quality_of_earnings.text: Analyze historical earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (“EBITDA”) at segment levels including understanding the impact of potential non-recurring or non-operating items such as:
Obtain and comment on the Seller’s proposed adjustments analysis;
Working with Client, understand potential impact with potential change in incentive compensation, dis-synergies, or synergies (if applicable);
Understand the impact of the Company’s JV business in the dredging sector;
The impact of significant accounting policies, provisions, management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
Other large, unusual or non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results;
Non-cash items such as the impact of provisions, management estimates, or adjusting entries on the reported results; and
Other potential items discovered in the due diligence process.

revenue_analysis.text: To the extent available, obtain and read an analysis of Target’s revenues and margins and inquire about the following:
Analyze revenue, margin, pricing and average project length on a project basis by the following (where available):

children.text: Revenue stream (civil work, special projects (buildings), services and equipment rental);
Customer
Geography
Industry

revenue_analysis.text: Understand Target’s job costing methodology (components included in margin, and periodic true ups)

Understand Target’s revenue recognition and billing process (e.g. percentage of completion, periodic and contract completion true-ups (and its variability), loss provision process, policies in place to control accounting recognition and policy escalations, and lifecycle), with specific focus on Construction Management contracts, and its corresponding timing impact to EBITDA;
Understanding the profile (and exposure) of major project/contracts (i.e. Indigenous Partnership with Target.);
Obtain and analyze the backlog and active backlog as at latest available date (backlog margin on a project basis, and analysis of historical backlog conversion)

working_capital.text: Obtain and read an analysis of working capital and inquire about the following on a combined basis:
Understand the composition of individual working capital accounts to determine sufficient working capital needs of the Company (including bonding requirements);
Monthly trending and fluctuations in working capital and impact on reported metrics (e.g. days sales/payables/inventory outstanding, net working capital as a percentage of revenues, etc.);

Large, unusual, or non-operating items and current or upcoming initiatives that may affect normalized working capital trends, such as exited business, settlements, capital projects, understanding non-trade balances, etc.
Gain an understanding of the nature of the Company’s unearned revenue.

prepaid_materials_and_other_assets.text: Prepare a summary of other assets (including prepaid materials, prepaid assets) and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances

inventory.text: Prepare a summary of inventory and obtain an understanding of the Company’s inventory costing methodology, tracking of inventory, work-in-progress and obsolescence reserve.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:

children.text: Terms of trade with major suppliers, significant vendor allowances, rebates and/or purchase commitments;
Trade payables aging analysis and days payable outstanding analysis;
Billings in excess of cost profile; and

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Obtain an understanding of the Company’s current equipment, including:

children.text: Mix of owned versus leased equipment/ vehicles;
Average age of equipment/ vehicles;
For leased fleet, understand the annual payment requirements;
For owned fleet, obtain details of historical capital expenditures and comment on planned, committed, and deferred capital expenditures.

net_debt.text: Prepare a summary of debt and debt-like items (e.g. notes payable, letters of credit, capital vendor payables, deferred revenue, pension deficit, warranty matters, exposure from onerous contracts, contingent matters, etc.), other assets, and liabilities and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.
Summary of lease right-of-use assets and liabilities.

related_parties.text: Inquire about Target’s transactions (e.g. income statement and balance sheet impacts) with related parties including subsidiaries, holding companies, joint ventures, variable interest entities, and other entities under common ownership.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Obtain and comment on any contingent claims or liabilities including:

children.text: Pending or threatened litigation or investigation by regulatory or other authorities; and
Other off-balance sheet transactions (including contingencies and guarantees).

quality_of_earnings.text: Provide a high-level analysis regarding the profit and loss performance of Target in the form of a quality of earnings analysis, summarizing the risks that may impact earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization ("EBITDA"), including:

children.text: Adjustments proposed by Target;
Cash-to-accrual adjustments;
By clinic analysis: Revenue and direct costs (such as doctor/compensation, rent) by location;
Revenue by type and pricing trends;
Per visit trends;
Physician production.

operating_expenses.text: Physician and employee compensation and related costs by function;
Rent;
Professional fees;
Any key supplier cost arrangements (i.e. volume rebates and minimum commitments, for top suppliers);
Unusual and extraordinary items;
Customer acquisition expense trends (e.g. Marketing, promotion and advertising);
Other general and administrative expenses.

quality_of_revenue_and_receivables_and_c.text: Using the underlying billing data, develop a revenue waterfall analysis and understand the following:

children.text: Historical collection profile;
Variances between implied accrual revenue and the Company's internal records; and
Accounts receivable profile.

quality_of_revenue_and_receivables_and_c.text: Compare the Company's cash receipts and disbursements to bank statement inflows and outflows

working_capital.text: Obtain details on Target's working capital and conduct the following:

children.text: Monthly working capital trends, including seasonality, for the past 24 months;

on factors that may have distorted the current or historical working capital position.

children.text: Unusual or non-standard contracts;

children.text: Same location and de novo center;
Center-based vs. home-based;
Type of procedure by CPT code;

Understands the health and sustainability of revenue and margin, that will bring clarity and insights into the drivers of business performance to strengthen deal conviction. Provides confidence in value creation planning / realization by uncovering opportunities earlier.

Store costs analyzed as a percentage of sales or applicable metric (selling payroll, other store payroll, store rent, marketing, shrinkage, sales-by-square footage, etc.).
Sales and COGS adjustments (including sales returns, markdowns, supplier revenue, stock shrinkage, obsolescence, other income, loyalty points and gift cards);

data_and_analytics.text: The purpose of this section is to perform detailed analysis on the drivers of revenue and margin growth, marketing performance, and operations to understand upside opportunities and communicate an effective story in conjunction with the Quality of Earnings. The outputs will be a PowerPoint trend report, highlighting key insights; a Power BI dashboard that / sell-side advisor can use to 'slice & dice' and extract visuals, this can also be shared with select buyers; and a consolidated Excel sales cube. Steps below are dependent on data from the brands

revenue_and_profitability_analysis_da.text: Understands the health and sustainability of revenue and margin; covers last fiscal years (the Historical Period) and TTM (the most recent period)

Leveraging proprietary analytics tool, use the transaction / sales database from each entity (business units), analyze revenue and profitability (cost, price, margin), key KPIs (e.g., number of jobs, average sales price, etc.) and performance trends (e.g., concentration, new-lost-existing customers, price-volume-mix, high growth services, OEM/equipment used, etc.); evaluating by product category/service offering, (e.g., Heating, Cooling, Plumbing, Electrical, etc.), sub-category/service type (e.g., AC installs, AC repairs, AC maintenance, etc.) and by channel (e.g., Google, Social Media, Direct Campaign, referral, etc.), by service type, by geography (zip code, county, etc.), by branch, by customer, by end market, etc.

Perform a service-level line item analysis to understand profitability (including price, volume, and mix calculations; low margin service areas; basket analysis, etc.), opportunities to optimize pricing, areas to rationalize, reflect cost increases, drive cross-sell / improve baskets, etc.
Extract and identify top brands / OEMs used in servicing customers and understand any opportunities to harmonize pricing across different business that might result in upside
Where available, link above sales / job data to technician utilization and salary to understand performance opportunities
Extract and understand any patterns and trends that might inform value creation and optimization opportunities for the next buyer

customer_base_health_da.text: Building on the previous step, the phase evaluates the health of the customer base; how sustainable and recurring (or re-occurring) in nature are the customers; covers last fiscal years (the Historical Period) and TTM (the most recent period)

Analyze health of the customer base across the three entities, in terms of revenue / profitability by customer, type of customer (residential, commercial, etc.) any customer contracts, etc.; overall retention, growth patterns, renewal history, cohort (vintage); new, loss, existing; customer concentration; etc. including associated services and categories these customers purchase to understand patterns and insights

Analyze type of revenue and mix (one-time versus recurring), across different streams (e.g., revenues from HVAC & Electrical, from Repair Services, from Extended Warranty, and Construction), and understand any performance trending insights
Extract and understand any patterns and trends that might inform value creation and optimization opportunities for the next buyer

marketing_and_advertising_performance_da.text: Evaluates the performance of marketing and advertising; data is analyzed in conjunction with the revenue and customer analysis to understand any trends / insights in relation to how marketing and advertising drives performance; covers last fiscal years (the Historical Period) and TTM (the most recent period)

Analyzes trends and insights related to revenue, customers, average revenue/margin per job, recurring nature of customers, high value products, etc. by lead source /channel by brand and advertising spend by lead source / channel by brand
Extracts insights into drivers of profitability and opportunities for value creation / optimization by the next buyer

Analyzes the sales funnel and conversion metrics by lead source / channel by brand, including leads generated, quotes, and sales conversion rates; ties this to revenue and customer analysis in the previous steps to understand insights to drive the business
Analyzes revenue generating job (e.g., service visit, install, replacement, etc.) and ties back to performance

operations_performance_da.text: Evaluates the performance of operations; where appropriate, ties back this analysis to the previous steps; covers last fiscal years (the Historical Period) and TTM (the most recent period)

Analyze employee census by role, position, annual salary, and bonus; where appropriate (e.g., technicians, sales people) tie back to revenue / margin performance to understand returns (e.g., salary vs sales or utilization multiples) and other associated metrics (e.g., revenue per tech, utilization, margin by tech, etc.)
Evaluate technician turnover metrics by brand and whether it is voluntary or involuntary; further evaluate against tenure (<90 days vs. >90 days); where appropriate tie back to sales and compensation

Analyze the fleet of each brand respectively, including number of vehicles, age of vehicles, lease vs. owned, maintenance cost per vehicle, average cost of replacement, etc.; where appropriate, tie back to technician performance (e.g., revenue / margin)

Analyze suppliers, including total purchases, type of material, typical markups to customers, etc.; understand rebates vs. volume discounts; where possible, tie back to job type (e.g., HVAC, electrical) and overall job performance (e.g., revenue / margin)

Assess financing partners, including the number of jobs that are finance, type, average selling price, financing partners, terms, etc.; where appropriate, tie back to salesperson, lead channel, etc. to understand opportunities to optimize

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Obtain and read an analysis of the Company's accounts payables and accrued liabilities and inquire about:

children.text: Terms of trade with major vendors, aging analysis;
Accrued liabilities, other current and non-current liabilities; and
Deferred revenue/unearned revenue profile and recognition.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Obtain and read an analysis of the Company's existing capital costs and inquire about:

children.text: Capitalized labour trends (labour, software development and commission); and
Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures.

net_debt.text: Commitment and contingencies.

audit_work_paper.text: Obtain and read audit working papers for the last two fiscal years and perform the following:
Comment on the nature and volume of audit differences (recorded and unrecorded adjustments), use of accounting estimates, any changes in accounting policies or methods; and
Read Management reports prepared by the external auditors and comment on control issues, and if applicable, discuss with auditor, accounting matters, or audit issues, if any.

business_overview.text: Understand Target’s accounting of intercompany transactions and consolidation process; and
Significant accounting policies and estimates and contemplated changes.

supporting_analysis_for_quality_of_earni.text: Obtain and read an analysis of sales and expenses, and enquire about, to the extent available:

children.text: Sales and COGS adjustments (including sales returns, markdowns, stock shrinkage, obsolescence, discounts, rebates, other income);
For Retail, understand sales and growth by tonnage;
For Wholesale, understand sales and growth by customer account
Store costs analyzed as a percentage of sales, square footage, or other applicable metric

supporting_analysis_for_quality_of_earni.text: Evaluate and understand the key trends and dynamics that impact sustainability of revenue and margins;
Evaluate product mix by revenue, costs, and margin to extract key trends that impact revenue forecasts and store performance for both the retail business and the wholesale business
Analyze cross-sell, upsell, retention; cohorts of product category and typical purchasing behaviors (e.g., bundling, etc.)
Understand impact of any private label brands on margin; any differences by location
Evaluate by location to understand gross margin contributions

Analyze business performance, trends, revenue, costs, and margin by location; including typical product category offered at locations that impact the differences in performance – informs locations to rationalize / expand
Evaluate local competitive presence (e.g., number of competitors within 5 or 10km radius circles) that might impact store performance
Integrate with insights from conducting product category analysis
Conduct a ramp-up analysis for recent locations to evaluate any relevant EBITDA adjustments that were identified
Analyze the health of the customer base, including purchasing patterns (e.g., every few days, or week, etc.) and typical purchased / bucketed categories
Leverage target’s customer data (e.g., anonymized) to analyze retention, growth, margin, average order side (AOS), impact of loyalty program, etc.
Analyze metrics such as order frequency, new customers as a percentage of active customers, etc. to understand the health of the customer base
Understand Target’s central production unit and the current sales it supports

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of the Target’s selling, general and administration expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Salaries and wages (including headcount by area and function, if possible);
General and administrative expenses;
Information systems expenses;
Corporate costs;
Extraordinary expenses;
Rent and leases; and
Miscellaneous and other expenses.

working_capital.text: Obtain details of the monthly net working capital and comment on the following for Retail, Wholesale, and Corporate Overhead to the extent they can be bifurcated:

children.text: The composition of individual working capital accounts (e.g. trade receivables, accounts payable and accruals, inventory and inventory provisions, etc.);
Monthly working capital trends and impact on reported metrics (e.g. days sales outstanding, inventory turns, days payables outstanding, net working capital as a percentage of revenues, etc.);

Large, unusual, or non-operating items and current or upcoming initiatives that may affect normal working capital trends, (such as accruals for bonuses, capital projects, understanding non-trade balances, etc.);
Working capital seasonality and implications on cash flow and working capital peg; and
Understanding the accounting impact of allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs, inventory reserve, reserves for sales returns and allowances, and other accruals requiring judgment.

net_debt.text: For each of Retail, Wholesale, and Corporate Overhead (to the extent they can be bifurcated):
Analyze reported current and non-current debt and other debt-like items on the balance sheet;
Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt;
Understand whether there are any off-balance sheet liabilities (e.g. incentive plans, legal claims, guarantees, purchase commitments, etc.); and
Other purchase commitments (operating leases) and contingencies (legal matters).

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Obtain details of historical capital expenditures, classified as growth and maintenance, by asset category, and comment on:
Understand Target’s real estate profile including owned vs leased;
Growth vs. maintenance capital expenditures, in particular for new store builds, renovations, e-Commerce, IT/Cybersecurity, DCs, and production units
Committed capital expenditures, including recent monthly spending versus budget; and
Historical and planned capital expenditures.

audit_work_paper.text: Access Target’s Historical Period audit work papers and identified audit matters including unrecorded audit differences, control matters, and other salient findings.

assistance_with_transaction_documentatio.text: Read relevant asset purchase agreement definitions (e.g. net working capital and debt-like item definitions and adjustment procedures).

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of the business, including:

children.text: Legal and organizational structure;
Revenue streams;
Cost base, employee base and infrastructure; and
Key management team.

business_overview.text: Understand the business' accounting function, including:

children.text: key accounting practices, policies, procedures, and methodologies;
internal financial controls; and
financial reporting.

business_overview.text: Gain a general understanding of the services, customers, operations and entities in the business, including:

children.text: Organization structure, including Target's position within the broader Parent;
Key revenue streams (Professional Services, Managed Services, Perpetual License, Third Party Revenue); and

Key milestones in the Target's recent history, including key operational and financial developments of note (new service offerings / revenue streams / geographical expansion, cost investment, changes in key management).

business_overview.text: Understand points of contact with the broader Parent, including intergroup transactions, shared services and resources, shared customers, and allocated costs.

accounting_overview.text: Gain an understanding of the business' finance function, financial reporting framework and internal control environment (e.g. finance team set-up and reporting, key financials systems used, use of external accountant, reliance on shared Parent resources).

Understand significant accounting policies applied and, where applicable, understand changes in accounting policies over the Historical Period and the potential financial impact. In particular, understand:

children.text: Revenue recognition, in particular recognition of recurring revenues and deferral of revenues;
Matching of revenue and costs (including deferral of staff sales commissions);
Capitalization policy;
Monthly vs year-end policies; and
Adjustments, entries, or areas of improvement suggested by the business' external accountant.

accounting_overview.text: Identify components of EBITDA which are incurred entirely within Target (i.e. stand-alone costs) and costs that are allocated to / from Parent, and compare to understanding of intra-group points of contact with broader Parent.

revenue_analysis.text: Understand the business' revenue streams and related accounting policies and practice. To include understanding of:

children.text: cash / billing cycle;
typical contract terms;
timing of price increases; and
calculation of deferred revenue.

revenue_analysis.text: Understand what work has been performed to reconcile reported revenues, cash income, and ARR.
Consider methodology used by the seller in presenting ARR and associated KPIs (upsell, downsell, churn, ARR per license / per logo, key account concentration)
identify key anomalies or items of concern relating to calculation methodology and / or resulting trends.

quality_of_earnings.text: Perform an analysis of adjusted EBITDA compared to free cash flow and set out key considerations in relation to EBITDA to cash flow conversion.
In support of the quality of earnings analysis, consider:

children.text: Information made available by the seller including vendor due diligence materials, internal and external financial statements or MD&A material, board presentations, management Q&A, and other relevant information provided;
Monthly trending of income statement captions;
Balance sheet support provided by the seller; and
The results of the quality of revenue analysis.

quality_of_earnings.text: Consider items with a full year EBITDA impact, and quantify where possible, including:

children.text: Latest ARR (including consideration of churned and booked-not-billed customers); and
Investment or changes in the cost base of the business.

quality_of_earnings.text: Present an adjusted income statement showing revenues, costs and EBITDA on an adjusted basis.
Consider the quantum of recurring non-capital cash costs outside of EBITDA in the Historical Period (e.g. capitalized labour costs relating to IT / R&D projects, etc.).

In the event that you determine that additional work is required to consider the appropriateness of the stand alone costs, the future target operating model, one-off separation costs and / or stranded costs, we will discuss this with you and agree additional procedures under separate cover.

children.text: Present an analysis of revenues split between one-time (e.g. implementation) and recurring (analysis to be performed by you).
Present an analysis and comment on the development of recurring revenues, including a reconciliation of revenues over time (new / lost / existing revenues) (analysis to be performed by you).

arr_drivers.text: Review a reconciliation of annual recurring revenues (ARR) per the underlying "customer cube" of subscription revenues to reported revenue and deferred revenue per the internal financial information (analysis to be performed by you).
Comment on trends in key KPIs for recurring revenues as monitored by management (e.g. ARR by product type, customer count, churn, volume, upsell).

Summarize and comment on customer retention (churn) rates for recurring revenue streams by customer, by cohort, and by type (professional service vs managed service) (analysis to be performed by you).

operating_expenses.text: Based on analysis performed by you:
Comment on the development of the revenue and gross profit of the business addressing:

children.text: Growth in revenues, including by customer, by product, and split between subscription vs other one-time revenues (such as set-up / consulting fees); and
Development of margins and relationship between direct costs and revenues.

operating_expenses.text: Consider the line item/detailed breakdown of COGS and operating expenses for the historical period addressing costs by function / division, and comment on key movements and drivers of changes (e.g. trends in headcount and cost per head by function) (analysis to be performed by you).

Present an analysis bridging EBITDA development from the Historical Period to TTM, summarizing the key drivers identified, such as price and volume metrics and/or profitability by revenue type / division (analysis to be performed by you).

working_capital.text: Consider the net working capital analysis provided by the seller, and consider a potential buy-side position. To consider:

children.text: Historical working capital turnover KPIs (e.g. DSO, DPO, deferred revenue days);
Accounting for key working capital balances including deferred revenue, accounts receivable and accounts payable;
Completeness of historical accruals and payables; and
Monthly vs annual accounting for judgmental balances such as bonuses.

working_capital.text: Understand cash and working capital cycle, including the impact of government RFPs and payment terms on working capital balances.
Consider the components of net working capital, monthly trends (including days), key drivers and seasonality (analysis to be performed by you).

Present and comment on the average level of working capital and its development over the Historical Period for consideration when setting a working capital target. Consider one off / non-business items in reported working capital, 'allocated' balances within working capital, and the potential impact of stand-alone working capital balances when Target is no longer part of the Parent (analysis to be performed by you).
Discuss whether additional net working capital adjustments might be considered, in addition to those already identified in your analysis.
Consider the aging of trade payables and receivables.

locked_box.text: Consider the locked box analysis provided by the seller, and consider a potential buy-side position. To consider:

children.text: Adjustments to working capital balances based on quality of earnings and net working capital analysis performed (e.g. calculation of deferred revenue; recoverability of accounts receivable);
Amounts owed to / owed from shareholders;
Tax exposures identified by your tax due diligence team; and
Other debt and debt-like balances that could be included in the locked box.

net_debt.text: Present an analysis of the balance sheet for Target as at the last two fiscal year ends and the latest available date.
Consider your analysis stratifying the components of the balance sheet into net working capital, net cash / debt balances, fixed assets, and 'other' balances (analysis to be performed by you).
Discuss whether additional net working capital adjustments / net debt adjustments might be considered, in addition to those already identified in your analysis.

Summarize and comment on net debt considerations as at the latest balance sheet date, including contingent liabilities (e.g. ongoing or pending litigation), constructive liabilities (e.g. unaccrued bonuses) and other obligations (e.g., deferred or committed capex) – including balances relating to the broader Parent that could remain in the legal entity being acquired.

phase_1_gaap_considerations.text: We will conduct a high-level analysis to identify and evaluate the differences between the target's financial statement results prepared in accordance with Spanish GAAP and US GAAP. This analysis will encompass the following:

children.text: Comparison of significant accounting policies and practices.
Identification of GAAP differences between Spanish GAAP and US GAAP
Assessment of the potential impact of these differences on the overall financial statements.

accounting_overview.text: Revenue recognition;
Significant accounting estimates (e.g. inventory costing, allowance for doubtful accounts, etc.);
Recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures or estimates and provisions.

revenue_analysis.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's revenue and margins and inquire about:

children.text: Revenue and profitability by significant customer;
Revenue and profitability by customer type;
Revenue and profitability by customer profile (e.g., related party vs. third party);
Revenue and profitability by trip type (e.g., tours, day trips, etc.);
Impact of seasonality, if any;
Any non-recurring components of revenue; and
Cut off procedures.
For each of the above items a) – d), inquire about key performance indicators such as volume (e.g., number of trips, etc.).

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Direct costs (labour and benefits, etc.);
Rent and utilities expense;
Historical operating expense trend as a % of revenue; and
Unusual and extraordinary items.

working_capital.text: Comment on working capital trends for the past 24 months;
Adjust working capital for any significant items which are considered to be non-recurring in nature;
Comment on the seasonality of working capital;
Comment on cash management procedures; and
Understand trade ratio trends.

accounts_receivable.text: Obtain and read an analysis of accounts receivable and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Aging analysis and turnover analysis; and
Allowance for uncollectible accounts, write-offs and reserves.

inventory.text: Obtain and read an analysis of inventory accounts and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Costing methodology;
Composition (e.g. raw materials, supplies, finished goods etc.); and
Turnover, aging and provisions.

other_assets_and_liabilities.text: Prepare a summary of other assets and liabilities (including prepaid expenses and deposits, deferred revenue and customer deposits) and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Obtain and read an analysis of historic and future capital cost requirements including:

children.text: Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures;
Historical replacement cycle and costs;
Utilization per unit; and
Impairment write-downs and other issues.

net_debt.text: Prepare a summary of debt and debt-like items (e.g. notes payable, letters of credit, capital vendor payables, etc.), other assets, and liabilities and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.

related_parties.text: Inquire about and summarize related party transactions including:

children.text: Nature and extent of related party transactions; and
Basis of pricing for arrangements with related parties.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Off-balance sheet liabilities;
Purchase commitments;
Operating and capital leases;
Pending or threatened litigation or investigations.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of the retail model and channels (stores, eCommerce, wholesale, etc.), including store footprint and geographic mix.
Understand merchandising strategy, product/category mix, seasonality, and promotional cadence.
Understand key vendors, sourcing model, and supply chain / distribution network.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze revenue and gross margin trends by channel and major product category.
Analyze price-volume-mix drivers including promotional activity, markdowns, returns, and discounting.
Assess customer metrics where available (traffic, conversion, basket size, repeat rate/loyalty).

operating_expenses.text: Understand store labor model and scheduling, including wage rates, staffing levels, turnover, and overtime.
Review occupancy costs (rent, CAM, utilities) and store-level profitability drivers.
Understand marketing and fulfillment costs (including shipping, last-mile, and returns processing).

working_capital.text: Analyze inventory turns, aged inventory, stock availability, and shrink/obsolescence trends.
Review vendor terms, rebates/allowances, and accounts payable practices.
Assess customer liabilities and other working capital items (gift cards, loyalty liabilities, customer deposits).

inventory.text: Obtain and read an analysis of inventory and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Inventory composition by category and location/channel;
Inventory valuation methodology and reserves;
Stock shrinkage, obsolescence, and write-downs; and
Returns, damages, and vendor return programs.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of the project/contracting model, including service lines, end markets, geographic footprint, and typical contract types.
Understand backlog, bidding pipeline, and key customers/projects, including customer concentration.
Understand subcontractor usage, sourcing/procurement practices, and key suppliers.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze revenue and gross margin trends by project type, geography, and major customer.
Understand contract pricing structures (fixed price, time and materials, unit price) and change order practices.
Assess seasonality and weather/field-operations impacts on revenue and margin.

accounts_receivable.text: Understand billing practices, including progress billings, retention, and contract assets/liabilities.
Review aging, collectability, disputes/claims, and change-order collectability.
Assess concentration and credit risk by customer and project.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Understand subcontractor payables, accrued costs, and cut-off practices.
Assess payroll/union accruals (if applicable) and warranty/defect accruals where relevant.
Review contingent liabilities related to claims, liquidated damages, and performance obligations.

work_in_progress.text: Obtain and review work-in-progress (WIP) reporting and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Percentage-of-completion methodology and inputs (cost-to-complete, estimated gross margin);
Under/over billings and their drivers;
Job-level margin fade/gain and change-order tracking; and
Backlog composition, burn-off, and conversion assumptions.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Bonding capacity and surety requirements (if applicable).
Warranty obligations, claims, and litigation, including project-specific disputes.
Purchase commitments and long-lead materials exposure.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of product lines, programs, customers (OEM/tier relationships), and regulatory/quality requirements.
Understand backlog, program life cycles, and ramp/production cadence.
Understand manufacturing footprint, capacity constraints, and supply chain dependencies.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze revenue and gross margin trends by program, customer, and product line.
Understand contract structures (long-term agreements, pricing escalators, penalties/claims) and revenue recognition approach.
Assess customer concentration, order volatility, and backlog conversion assumptions.

inventory.text: Obtain and read an analysis of inventory/WIP and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Long-cycle inventory, work-in-process, and slow-moving/obsolete inventory;
Material price variance, scrap/rework, and yield;
Supplier lead times and risk of shortages; and
Inventory valuation and reserves.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Understand capex requirements for tooling, equipment, and capacity expansion.
Assess maintenance capex versus growth capex and related replacement cycles.
Review capitalized development/tooling costs (if applicable) and impairment considerations.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of the product and service portfolio, customer segments, and go-to-market channels.
Understand network/infrastructure model (owned vs leased) and key third-party dependencies.
Understand key commercial arrangements (carriers/partners, wholesale, roaming, etc.) where applicable.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze revenue trends by product line and customer segment, including recurring vs usage-based components.
Assess churn, ARPU, net adds, retention, and other subscriber/customer KPIs where available.
Understand pricing, discounting, promotions, and contract terms driving revenue sustainability.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Assess capex intensity and planned network/infrastructure investments.
Understand timing and drivers of capex programs and any deferral/backlog dynamics.
Review lease-versus-buy considerations for infrastructure and related cash flow impacts.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Review key long-term commitments (network leases, tower/site leases, capacity commitments, vendor agreements).
Assess regulatory and licensing exposure (including spectrum-related commitments if applicable).
Review material customer credits, service-level penalties, and dispute exposure.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of key products and customer segments (deposit, lending, payments, wealth, etc.).
Understand balance sheet composition, funding sources, and interest rate sensitivity drivers.
Understand regulatory environment and reporting requirements applicable to the business.

accounting_overview.text: Understand the financial reporting framework and key accounting policies, including fair value measurement and expected credit loss methodology (if applicable).
Understand key estimates and judgments (credit losses, valuation of financial instruments, impairments, litigation reserves).
Understand intercompany funding/transfer pricing and allocation practices (if applicable).

loan_portfolio_and_credit_quality.text: Obtain and analyze loan portfolio composition by product, geography, industry, and risk rating.
Review delinquency, non-performing assets, charge-offs, recoveries, and vintage/loss trends.
Assess concentrations (single-name, sector, geographic) and underwriting/credit policy changes over the Historical Period.

allowance_for_credit_losses.text: Understand allowance methodology, inputs, and model governance (if applicable).
Review qualitative overlays, management adjustments, and sensitivity to macro assumptions.
Reconcile allowance movements over time and assess coverage ratios versus peer/portfolio risk.

regulatory_capital_and_liquidity.text: Understand regulatory capital metrics and drivers (risk-weighted assets, capital buffers) where applicable.
Review liquidity profile and stress testing framework where applicable.
Assess the impact of off-balance sheet exposures and commitments on capital/liquidity.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of lines of business, distribution channels, and geographic footprint.
Understand premium growth drivers, retention, and pricing actions over the Historical Period.
Understand regulatory environment and reporting requirements applicable to the business.

accounting_overview.text: Understand key accounting policies and estimates related to reserves, premium recognition, and reinsurance.
Understand claim development patterns and reserving governance, including management judgment and assumptions.
Understand fair value measurement and investment accounting policies where applicable.

underwriting_and_loss_reserves.text: Analyze loss ratios, expense ratios, combined ratio, and key drivers by line of business.
Review reserve adequacy and development by accident/underwriting year and line of business.
Assess pricing adequacy, underwriting policy changes, and exposure to large losses/catastrophes where applicable.

claims_and_reinsurance.text: Understand claims handling processes, claim settlement patterns, and operational KPIs where available.
Review reinsurance program structure, retention levels, counterparty exposure, and recoverables.
Assess concentrations in ceded exposure and any disputes or collectability issues with reinsurers.
